entry.description: Entry is the Log entry information

info.description: Info is the log entry information.

startedAt.description: StartedAt is the start time of the connection/request.

endedAt.description: EndedAt is the end time of the connection/request.

reason.description: Reason sets the details as to why the request is allowed or denied (e.g. the Policy that triggered the decision)

policy.description: Policy is the standalone Policy whose rule match triggered the decision

inlinePolicy.description: InlinePolicy is the inline Policy of a resource whose rule match triggered the decision

resourceRef.description: ResourceRef is the reference to the Resource whose inline Policy triggered the rule match

name.description: Name is the name of the inline Policy if available

policyRef.description: PolicyRef is the reference to the Policy

tcp.description: TCP sets the TCP-specific entry details

http.description: HTTP sets the HTTP-specific entry details

ssh.description: SSH sets the SSH-specific entry details

udp.description: UDP sets the UDP-specific entry details

postgres.description: Postgres sets the PostgreSQL-specific entry details

kubernetes.description: Kubernetes sets the Kubernetes-specific entry details

grpc.description: GRPC sets the gRPC-specific entry details

mysql.description: MySQL sets the MySQL-specific entry details

dns.description: DNS sets the DNS-specific entry details

http.description: HTTP shows the underlying HTTP information of the request/response

serviceFullName.description: ServiceFullName is the full name of the Service (i.e. "<package>.<service>")

request.description: Request is the HTTP request information.

response.description: Response is the HTTP response information.

path.description: Path is the request's path without the query params.

userAgent.description: UserAgent is the value of the `User-Agent` request header.

method.description: Method is the request HTTP method.

referer.description: Referer is the value of the `Referer` request header.

scheme.description: Scheme is th scheme portion of the request's URI.

bodyBytes.description: BodyBytes is the size of the HTTP request body in bytes.

forwardedHost.description: ForwardedHost represents the value of the X-Forwarded-Host header.

uri.description: URI is the request path including the query params

bodyMap.description: BodyMap is the serialized, usually JSON, body content

origin.description: Origin is the value of the Origin header

headers.description: Headers is the map of request headers, when enabled.

bodyBytes.description: BodyBytes is the size of the HTTP response body in bytes.

bodyMap.description: BodyMap is the serialized, usually JSON, body content

contentType.description: ContentType is the value of the Content-Type header

headers.description: Headers is the map of request headers, when enabled.

http.description: HTTP shows the underlying HTTP information of the request/response

name.description: Name is the resource name if available

start.description: Starts shows the details of the start of a PostgreSQL connection

query.description: Query shows the details of the query message

parse.description: Parse shows the details of the parse message

name.description: Name is the name in the parse message

query.description: Query is the query in the parse message
Query is the query in the query message

user.description: User is the effective user used to connect to the upstream server

database.description: Database is the effective database used to connect to the upstream server

requestedUser.description: RequestedUser is the user requested by the downstream. Maybe overridden according to the Service configuration

requestedDatabase.description: RequestedDatabase is the database requested by the downstream. Maybe overridden according to the Service configuration

applicationName.description: ApplicationName is the application_name parameter set by the downstream in the initial startup message

start.description: Start shows the details of the new SSH connection

directTCPIPStart.description: DirectTCPIPStart shows the details of the requested "direct-tcpip" channel

sessionRecording.description: SessionRecording shows the details and a session recording

sessionRequestExec.description: SessionRequestExec shows the details of a session exec request

sessionRequestSubsystem.description: SessionRequestSubsystem shows the details of a session subsystem request

host.description: Host is the destination host of the "direct-tcpip" channel

port.description: Port is the destination port of the "direct-tcpip" channel

data.description: Data is the data content of the recording chunk

command.description: Command is the command of the exec request

name.description: Name is the name of the subsystem request

requestedUser.description: RequestedUser is the requested SSH user by the downstream.

user.description: User is the effective SSH user actually used to connect to the upstream server

receivedBytes.description: ReceivedBytes is the number of bytes received from downstream. Only used with END entries.

sentBytes.description: SentBytes is the number of bytes sent to downstream. Only used with END entries.

id.description: ID is the unique ID for the log entry

createdAt.description: CreatedAt is the timestamp of creation.

actorRef.description: Actor is the actor whose action triggered the log entry (i.e. the User, their Session and Device if available)

targetRef.description: Target is the target of the action that triggered the log entry (e.g. the Service and its Namespace in the case of a SERVICE log entry)

apiVersion.description: APIVersion is the API version of the reference resource

kind.description: Kind is the resource kind. A kind is unique per an APIVersion.

uid.description: UID is the resource object UID. A UID is unique across the entire Cluster.

name.description: Name is the resource object name. A name is unique per a certain kind.

resourceVersion.description: ResourceVersion is the resourceVersion of the resource.
